title: Tenderly Roasted Ethiopian Yellow Pepper Injera
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring roasted yellow peppers and injera, cooked to perfection on a robata grill using binchotan charcoal for a smoky flavor, and made with macrobiotic friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit using binchotan charcoal for a smoky flavor.
Cut the yellow peppers into large pieces and brush with olive oil.
Season the peppers with salt, black pepper, and berbere spice.
Place the peppers on the robata grill and roast for 30 minutes, turning occasionally.
While the peppers are roasting, heat a few pieces of injera bread on the grill for a few minutes on each side.
Once the peppers are tender, remove them from the grill and let them cool.
Chop the cooled peppers into smaller pieces and mix with minced garlic and chopped onion.
Serve the pepper mixture on top of the grilled injera bread.
